text: Elon Musk is the CEO of Tesla and founded SpaceX in 2002.
Albert Einstein was born in Ulm, Germany in 1879 and developed the theory of relativity.
Apple Inc. was founded by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne in Cupertino, California.
The Python programming language was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991.
Microsoft acquired GitHub in 2018 for $7.5 billion.
Marie Curie was a physicist and chemist who won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903 and the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1911.
The Eiffel Tower is located in Paris, France. It was designed by Gustave Eiffel and built between 1887 and 1889.
OpenAI was founded in 2015 by Sam Altman, Elon Musk, and others. It created ChatGPT and GPT-4.
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is a subsidiary of Amazon that provides cloud computing services.
The Great Wall of China was built over many centuries by various Chinese dynasties to protect against invasions.
Barack Obama served as the 44th President of the United States from 2009 to 2017.
Netflix was founded by Reed Hastings and Marc Randolph in 1997 in Scotts Valley, California.
Isaac Newton formulated the laws of motion and universal gravitation, which dominated scientific views for nearly three centuries.
The World Health Organization (WHO) is a specialized agency of the United Nations responsible for international public health.
Sundar Pichai is the CEO of Alphabet Inc. and its subsidiary Google.
The Roman Empire reached its greatest extent under Emperor Trajan in 117 AD.
Bitcoin was created by the pseudonymous person or group known as Satoshi Nakamoto in 2008.
The Amazon River flows through Brazil and is the world's largest river by discharge volume.
J.K. Rowling wrote the Harry Potter series, which was published by Bloomsbury Publishing.
Toyota Motor Corporation is a Japanese multinational automotive manufacturer headquartered in Toyota City, Aichi, Japan.
The Hubble Space Telescope was launched in 1990 and is operated by NASA and the European Space Agency.
Winston Churchill served as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom during World War II.
The Mediterranean Sea borders Southern Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia.
Ludwig van Beethoven was a German composer and pianist. His Symphony No. 9 is considered one of his greatest works.
The International Space Station is a modular space station in low Earth orbit and is a joint project among five space agencies.
Facebook was founded by Mark Zuckerberg along with fellow Harvard College students in 2004.
The Nile River flows northward through northeastern Africa and empties into the Mediterranean Sea.
Charles Darwin developed the theory of evolution by natural selection, published in 'On the Origin of Species' in 1859.
Samsung Electronics is a South Korean multinational electronics corporation and subsidiary of Samsung Group.
The United Nations was established in 1945 after World War II to promote international cooperation.
Tim Cook became CEO of Apple after Steve Jobs resigned in 2011.
The Sahara Desert covers most of North Africa and is the world's largest hot desert.
William Shakespeare was an English playwright and poet, widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language.
The Large Hadron Collider is operated by CERN and is located on the border of France and Switzerland.
Spotify was founded in 2006 in Stockholm, Sweden by Daniel Ek and Martin Lorentzon.
The Taj Mahal is an ivory-white marble mausoleum in Agra, India, commissioned by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan.
Nikola Tesla was a Serbian-American inventor best known for his contributions to the design of the modern alternating current electricity supply system.
The FIFA World Cup is held every four years and is the most watched sporting event in the world, organized by FIFA.
SpaceX successfully launched the Falcon Heavy rocket in 2018, making it the most powerful operational rocket at the time.
The Louvre Museum in Paris, France houses the Mona Lisa painting by Leonardo da Vinci.
Alibaba Group was founded by Jack Ma in Hangzhou, China in 1999.
The Apollo 11 mission landed astronauts Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin on the Moon in 1969.
Vincent van Gogh was a Dutch post-impressionist painter. His famous works include The Starry Night.
The European Union is a political and economic union of 27 member states located primarily in Europe.
Alphabet Inc. is the parent company of Google, formed in 2015 through a restructuring of Google.
Alan Turing is considered the father of computer science and artificial intelligence.
CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing technology was developed by Jennifer Doudna and Emmanuelle Charpentier.
The Great Barrier Reef is located in the Coral Sea off the coast of Queensland, Australia.
Uber was founded in 2009 by Travis Kalanick and Garrett Camp in San Francisco, California.
The Colosseum in Rome, Italy was built under Emperor Vespasian and completed in 80 AD under Titus.
